cards.name: Satura Cakes
cards.address: 200 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Satura Cakes on Main Street in Los Altos for an afternoon treat. The pastry case is stacked — grab a slice of cake or a few cookies for the kids to snack on.
cards.why: A quick, sweet pitstop on a cool afternoon with the family hits just right.

cards.name: Linden Tree Children's Books
cards.address: 265 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Walk over to Linden Tree Children's Books on State Street and let the kids browse. It's the kind of independent bookshop where everyone ends up finding something they didn't know they needed.
cards.why: Los Altos's best kept secret for families — kids go in skeptical and come out begging for three books.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Smoking Pig BBQ in San Jose for dinner. Get the brisket plate, grab some mac and cheese, and let the kids go to town. It's casual, it's messy, and it's exactly what a Wednesday evening should be.
cards.why: Best BBQ in the South Bay and the price won't hurt — low-key perfect for a family dinner.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through exhibits tracing the full arc of computing history, from punch cards to pocket-sized supercomputers. The interactive displays make it easy to lose track of time.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fascinating place to spend a Wednesday evening when the crowds are lighter than weekends.

cards.name: Sorelle Italian Bistro
cards.address: 915 S San Tomas Aquino Rd, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Settle in for housemade pasta, wood-fired dishes, and a warm neighborhood atmosphere at this beloved Campbell Italian bistro.
cards.why: A cozy dinner spot that hits the right note on a cool spring evening without feeling too formal.

cards.name: Slingshot Pinball
cards.address: 460 Lincoln Ave #70, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Cap the night flipping bumpers and racking up points on a curated lineup of vintage and modern pinball machines in a lively arcade bar setting.
cards.why: It's a fun, low-key way to wind down the evening with something a little different than the usual bar scene.

cards.name: Keke's Breakfast Cafe
cards.address: 311 S Mathilda Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Keke's with a solid breakfast before the day gets going — eggs, pancakes, the works.
cards.why: A proper sit-down breakfast sets the tone for a good Wednesday.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Get the brisket plate at Smoking Pig BBQ and don't skip the mac and cheese — this is the real deal South Bay BBQ spot.
cards.why: You burned the calories, now you earn this.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the sculpture garden and browse the contemporary galleries at Triton Museum of Art — it's completely free and genuinely impressive for a local museum.
cards.why: Free world-class art on a cloudy afternoon is an easy yes.

cards.name: Pints of Joy
cards.address: 717 E El Camino Real #6, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Pints of Joy in Sunnyvale for a scoop or two — rotating flavors, good quality, worth the detour.
cards.why: A little treat between the museum and dinner is never a bad call.

cards.name: Heroes & Champions
cards.address: 574 E El Camino Real, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Heroes and Champions for comics, graphic novels, and collectibles — the kind of shop you can easily lose an hour in.
cards.why: It's right there and way more fun than scrolling your phone between stops.

cards.name: Focus Karaoke & Cafe
cards.address: 1336 Saratoga Ave, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a private room and belt out your favorite songs with friends — Focus Karaoke & Cafe has a solid song library and food and drinks you can order right to your room to keep the energy going all night.
cards.why: It's a fun, low-key way to cap off the evening after all the eating and exploring you've done today.

cards.name: Ed R. Levin County Park
cards.address: 3100 Calaveras Rd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Ed Levin is a solid afternoon stretch — wide open grassland, creek views, and enough trail to actually feel like you went somewhere without overdoing it.
cards.why: Cool enough weather for a walk and the hills look great when it's partly cloudy.

cards.name: Tea Top Milpitas
cards.address: 82 Ranch Dr, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with a tea or milk tea at Tea Top — decent menu, chill vibe, good spot to sit and decompress after the walk.
cards.why: Exactly the right kind of low-key stop for a Wednesday afternoon.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Smoking Pig for dinner and get the brisket plate. The mac and cheese is not optional.
cards.why: Honest BBQ, reasonable prices, and you'll leave genuinely full.

cards.name: Student Candlelight Mass
cards.blurb: The Student Candlelight Mass at Santa Clara University is a quiet, beautiful way to cap the evening. The setting alone is worth showing up for.
cards.why: Free, and completely different from anything else you'd do on a Wednesday night.

cards.name: EcoFashion Show 2026
cards.blurb: An EcoFashion show is genuinely more fun than it sounds — expect bold design, sustainable materials, and student designers who actually have something to say.
cards.why: Free, creative, and a solid way to kick off the evening without overthinking it.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Smoking Pig BBQ and get the brisket plate. The mac and cheese is not optional.
cards.why: It's some of the best BBQ in the South Bay and the price won't hurt.

cards.name: Magitek Games
cards.address: 1122 Cadillac Ct, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Magitek Games opens at 6 and runs late, so this is the right time to show up. Good tabletop selection and a crowd that actually knows what they're doing.
cards.why: A Wednesday night here beats sitting at home, and Milpitas is already where you're ending up.

cards.name: Manley’s Donuts
cards.address: 2590 S Bascom Ave, San Jose, CA 95124, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Manley's for a classic donut run — grab a glazed or two for the kids and a coffee for yourself before the day kicks off.
cards.why: A no-fuss, cheap start that the kids will love.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: The Los Gatos Creek Trail is flat, easy, and shaded — perfect for little legs. Walk south from Dell Ave along the creek and let the kids burn some energy while you enjoy the trees and water.
cards.why: Easy terrain, no cost, and genuinely pretty on a cool spring morning.

cards.name: Square Pie Guys
cards.address: 501 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Square Pie Guys does Detroit-style pizza with crispy edges and a thick, cheesy base. Order a couple of squares and let the kids pick their toppings — it's the kind of lunch everyone actually agrees on.
cards.why: Detroit-style pizza is a guaranteed crowd-pleaser, and this is one of the best versions in the South Bay.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: The Computer History Museum is legitimately wild for kids — robots, old computers the size of rooms, and interactive stuff throughout. Check out the Read Me, My Year With AI, and The Future of Humanoid Robots exhibitions while you're in there. Budget a solid two to three hours and let the kids lead the way.
cards.why: It's hands-on enough to keep kids engaged and interesting enough that you'll actually enjoy it too.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Smoking Pig BBQ is the move for dinner. Get the brisket plate, load up on mac and cheese, and don't stress about the kids making a mess — this is that kind of place.
cards.why: It's affordable, casual, and the BBQ is genuinely the best in the South Bay.

cards.name: Books Inc.
cards.address: 1875 S Bascom Ave #600, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Cap the night at Books Inc. in Campbell. Let the kids pick out a book each, browse the shelves yourself, and enjoy a relaxed end to the day without screens.
cards.why: A chill, kid-friendly way to wind down without rushing anyone to bed.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: The Computer History Museum is genuinely wild for kids — interactive exhibits, giant old computers, and enough 'wait, that's how it worked?' moments to keep everyone engaged for a couple of hours.
cards.why: It's hands-on enough to hold a kid's attention and interesting enough that you'll actually enjoy it too.

cards.name: The Future of Humanoid Robots
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: While you're already at the museum, swing through the Humanoid Robots exhibition before it wraps for the day — kids will lose their minds over the life-size robots on display.
cards.why: Same building, same ticket, and robots are an automatic win with kids.

cards.name: Naans & Curries
cards.address: 102 Great Mall Dr Suite 102, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Naans and Curries at Great Mall is the move for dinner — the naan is fresh and the curries are rich without being too spicy for kids.
cards.why: A top-rated local spot that hits the spot after a busy afternoon.

cards.name: Mega Claws
cards.address: 979 Story Rd Unit 7027, San Jose, CA 95122, USA
cards.blurb: Mega Claws is a claw machine arcade on Story Road — easy to kill an hour here while the kids try their luck and burn off that post-dinner energy.
cards.why: Affordable, low-key fun that kids actually love, and it stays open late enough to finish the night on a high note.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 1140 Lincoln Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Pick out a piece to paint and get the kids set up at a table — Petroglyph lets everyone work at their own pace, so no stress if the little one takes forever on their mug.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun hands-on activity that keeps kids focused and happy for a solid hour.

cards.name: San Jose Earthquakes vs. Austin FC
cards.address: 1123 Coleman Ave, San Jose
cards.blurb: Head to PayPal Park for the Earthquakes vs. Austin FC match — grab some stadium snacks, find your seats, and let the kids go nuts cheering.
cards.why: Live soccer is a great time with kids, and the stadium atmosphere does all the work for you.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a quick plate of smoky, fall-off-the-bone BBQ — the pulled pork and ribs are the move — before heading over to catch the Earthquakes match.
cards.why: It's a solid, affordable bite right in the neighborhood to fuel you up for the game.

cards.name: GK Pastry & Sourdough
cards.address: 251 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at GK Pastry and grab a coffee and something from the pastry case — the sourdough is the real draw here, but the baked goods go fast so get there and pick something before they're gone.
cards.why: Best morning coffee stop in the area, no contest.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk or jog the Los Gatos Creek Trail — it's flat, shaded, and follows the creek the whole way, so even on a gray morning it feels good to be out here.
cards.why: Cool weather like this is basically perfect for a trail walk.

cards.name: Yiassoo
cards.address: 10660 S De Anza Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Hit Yiassoo for lunch and work through the Greek menu — the rice dishes and grilled plates are solid, and the portions are generous enough that you won't need to snack for hours.
cards.why: It's a genuinely good lunch spot and exactly the right call after a morning walk.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Spend the afternoon at the Triton Museum of Art — wander the galleries and make sure you get out to the sculpture garden, which is worth the visit on its own even when the weather is iffy.
cards.why: Free admission and genuinely great contemporary art make this an easy yes.

cards.name: EcoFashion Show 2026
cards.blurb: Catch the EcoFashion Show 2026 at Santa Clara University — it's a student-produced fashion show built around sustainable design, and the creativity on display tends to be a lot more interesting than you'd expect.
cards.why: Free, local, and actually fun — a good way to spend that early evening gap.

cards.name: Dio Deka
cards.address: 210 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night at Dio Deka in Los Gatos — it's the nicest dinner in town, so dress up a little and settle in for a proper Greek meal with a good glass of wine on the patio if the weather cooperates.
cards.why: You earned a real dinner after a full day, and this place delivers.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through the full Revolution exhibit and let yourself get sucked into the timeline of how we got from punch cards to pocket supercomputers. Give yourself the full two hours — there's more here than it looks.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fascinating museum even if you've never written a line of code.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Stretch your legs on the paved trail that runs along Los Gatos Creek. It's flat, shaded in spots, and an easy hour-ish loop from the Dell Ave trailhead.
cards.why: Good way to shake off the museum and get some fresh air before dinner.

cards.name: Dio Deka
cards.address: 210 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a table on the patio at Dio Deka and start with cocktails while you browse the menu. The mezze spread is a solid opener before you commit to an entree. Dress up a little — it's worth it.
cards.why: Los Gatos has a lot of decent restaurants, but this one is actually special.

cards.name: PanIQ Escape Room San Jose
cards.address: 2270 Quimby Rd, San Jose, CA 95122, USA
cards.blurb: Book a room at PanIQ and spend 60 minutes locked in with your group trying to puzzle your way out. They have multiple themes, so pick whatever sounds most fun when you book.
cards.why: It's a solid way to end the night — competitive, fun, and you'll be talking about it after.

cards.name: MCA Bookstore
cards.address: 3003 Scott Blvd, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the shelves at MCA Bookstore — it's a genuinely good independent shop open into the evening, perfect for picking up something you didn't know you needed.
cards.why: A low-key, no-pressure way to kick off the evening.

cards.name: Dio Deka
cards.address: 210 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Dio Deka is the move for a proper sit-down dinner. Dress up a little, grab a patio table if the evening cooperates, and work through the menu slowly — this is the kind of Greek food that makes you rethink the cuisine.
cards.why: It's a Wednesday, which means the patio won't be slammed and the vibe will be exactly right.

cards.name: Arawan Thai Massage and Spa
cards.address: 14107 Winchester Blvd Suite J, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening with a relaxing Thai massage or spa treatment — a great way to decompress after a full evening of shopping and dining.
cards.why: It's right in Los Gatos, so you can slip in a little self-care before heading home.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio Open Space
cards.address: Rancho San Antonio County Park
cards.blurb: Start the morning on the trails at Rancho San Antonio. The lower loop is easy enough for little legs and winds through meadows with deer sightings almost guaranteed. Layers help since it'll be cool under the trees.
cards.why: It's the best free morning activity in the South Bay and the kids will love spotting wildlife.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Head into the Computer History Museum and let the kids loose on the exhibits. The Revolution exhibit walks through computing history in a way that's genuinely engaging, and the hands-on sections keep younger visitors busy.
cards.why: It's the kind of museum where the adults get just as absorbed as the kids.

cards.name: UMe | Tea & Snacks
cards.address: 220 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into UMe on Castro Street for bubble tea and snacks. It's a relaxed spot to sit down, recharge, and let everyone pick their drink before the afternoon stretch.
cards.why: The kids will want bubble tea and honestly you will too after a morning of hiking and museums.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Swing through the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara. The sculpture garden out back is wide open for kids to wander and the contemporary galleries inside are worth a slow loop. Best of all it's completely free.
cards.why: Free art museum with outdoor space is the perfect low-key afternoon move.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the outdoor plaza at Main Street Cupertino. It's a pleasant open-air stretch with places to grab a snack or coffee while the kids have room to move around before dinner.
cards.why: Good spot to stretch your legs and decompress between afternoon and dinner.

cards.name: Rocco's Ballpark
cards.address: 2531 Newhall St, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the evening at Rocco's Ballpark with arcade games, skee-ball, and a casual bite — a laid-back spot where you can unwind with drinks and friendly competition after a full day of culture and shopping.
cards.why: It's a low-key, fun way to wind down the evening right in Santa Clara after your Cupertino stop.

cards.name: Kids Knitting (Ages 7+)
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Drop the kids into this free knitting session at the Los Gatos Library — ages 7 and up get to learn the basics and take home something they actually made.
cards.why: Free, hands-on, and the kids will be proud of what they make.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Vasona Lake after the knitting wraps up — walk the path around the lake, let the kids stretch their legs, and catch the little train if it's still running.
cards.why: It's one of the best family parks in the South Bay and easy to squeeze in before dinner.

cards.name: Aqui Campbell
cards.address: 201 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Grab dinner at Aqui on Campbell Avenue. Fresh California-Mexican food, good portions, and a relaxed vibe that works great with kids in tow.
cards.why: Campbell Ave is a nice spot to wind down the evening and the food is solid across the board.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening with a stroll along this smooth, well-lit trail that follows Los Gatos Creek through Campbell — perfect for stretching your legs after dinner and catching the last of the daylight.
cards.why: It's a relaxed way to close out the day right near where you're already eating.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Get the brisket plate and load up on mac and cheese — Smoking Pig does BBQ better than anywhere else in the South Bay, and the portions are generous enough for hungry kids.
cards.why: Casual, affordable, and genuinely great food to kick off the evening.

cards.name: San Jose Earthquakes vs. Austin FC
cards.address: 1123 Coleman Ave, San Jose
cards.blurb: Head over to PayPal Park for the Earthquakes vs. Austin FC match. Grab a spot in the supporters section or find family-friendly seats on the sides, and let the kids go full chaos cheering on the home team.
cards.why: Live soccer is a blast with kids and tickets start at $20, so it's a genuinely easy yes.

cards.name: Trials Pub
cards.address: 265 N First St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the night at this beloved British-style pub — grab a pint, settle into the relaxed atmosphere, and replay the Earthquakes match over some pub snacks.
cards.why: It's steps from PayPal Park and the perfect low-key cap to a big game night.
